item.title: The Goldilocks Principle in Fantasy Strategy 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on their deep attachment to Heroes of Might and Magic II, played extensively during the pandemic, and contrasts it with Heroes III, which they enjoyed less due to darker art and added complexity. They explore why fantasy strategy appeals—its unreality avoids ethical discomfort with real-world conquest. The article then surveys three turn-based fantasy strategy games from the late 1990s: Warlords III, Age of Wonders, and Disciples: Sacred Lands. It details Warlords III's development by Steve Fawkner and Strategic Studies Group, noting its automated combat and rudimentary graphics, as a starting point for finding similar games.
标签: Heroes of Might and Magic, fantasy strategy, turn-based strategy, Warlords III, Strategic Studies Group, game design, retro gaming, 4X games, art style, AI in games

item.title: Jerry Seinfeld Tries Out Snap’s Specs 摘要
item.description:
Jerry Seinfeld tries out Snap's Spectacles, with a humorous reference to Seinfeld's father also trying them. The article is a brief link post from Daring Fireball, highlighting the cultural moment of a celebrity testing the smart glasses.
标签: Snap, Spectacles, Jerry Seinfeld, smart glasses, tech, celebrity, wearables, AR, Daring Fireball

item.title: Snap Launches Ad Campaign for Specs Starring Michael Caine 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses Snap's new ad campaign for its Spectacles AR glasses, featuring actor Michael Caine. The author reflects on Caine's quote about glasses being about power and the powerful people who wear them, while questioning whether the Spectacles are stylish despite potential discomfort to ears. The piece links to a PCMag article criticizing the design.
标签: Snap, Spectacles, AR glasses, ad campaign, Michael Caine, wearable tech, design, fashion, tech criticism

item.title: The data black hole at the center of AI 摘要
item.description:
The article argues that AI progress is primarily driven by data scale rather than sample efficiency improvements. It defines intelligence as sample efficiency, noting that current AI models require millions of times more data than humans to learn tasks. The author highlights the massive human expert data needed for RL training, comparing it to a 'black hole of data.' Despite poor sample efficiency, AI labs focus on automating common white-collar tasks where data abundance compensates. The post questions whether current AIs can achieve human-level learning without fundamental breakthroughs, suggesting future AI researchers might solve this. Comparisons with human learning and objections about evolution, multimodal data, and scaling laws are discussed.
标签: AI, sample efficiency, data scaling, reinforcement learning, human vs AI learning, white-collar automation, scaling laws, RL data generation, intelligence definition, AI progress

item.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2) 摘要
item.description:
The article critiques the Silicon Valley AI bubble, focusing on OpenAI's massive losses ($21 billion in 2025 on $13.07 billion revenue) and questionable accounting practices. It argues that the industry has shifted from building real value to protecting venture capital investments through hype and fear-mongering. The author contends that AI companies promote unrealistic promises of sentient computers and infinite growth while ignoring current economic realities. The piece warns that this bubble exploits intellectual weaknesses across organizations, distorting incentives and eliminating guardrails against reckless exuberance, with potential severe economic consequences.
标签: AI, Silicon Valley, bubble, OpenAI, venture capital, hype, economics, tech industry, financials, critique

item.title: Fox to Buy Roku Streaming Service in $25 Billion Deal 摘要
item.description:
Fox Corp. announced it will acquire Roku for approximately $25 billion, marking Fox's largest deal to date. The acquisition combines Fox's live news and sports programming with Roku's connected TV platform, aiming to strengthen Fox's streaming business alongside its existing services like Tubi, Fox One, and Fox Nation. The combined entity will compete for ad dollars against Amazon and Netflix. However, the article criticizes the deal, arguing that Roku's market share is based on user indifference rather than loyalty, and predicts its share will decline.
标签: Fox, Roku, acquisition, streaming, advertising, media, TV, business, deal, criticism

item.title: Trump Mobile T1 Phone Is a Gold-Painted Two-Year-Old HTC U24 Pro 摘要
item.description:
NBC News and iFixit report that the Trump Mobile T1 phone, marketed as 'Made in the USA,' is actually a gold-painted version of the two-year-old HTC U24 Pro, which is made by Taiwanese company HTC using Chinese parts. The T1 costs $500, only slightly more than the HTC-branded phone at $470. iFixit's teardown confirms the rebranding, with the only difference being the paint job.
标签: Trump Mobile T1, HTC U24 Pro, iFixit, rebranding, smartphone, Made in USA, teardown, NBC News, consumer electronics, misleading marketing

item.title: Full Page Paralysis 摘要
item.description:
The author argues that starting a project is easy, but finishing is the hard part. They contrast 'blank page paralysis' with 'full page paralysis'—the difficulty of completing and publishing work. Finishing makes something real and subject to judgment, which creates fear of imperfection. The author relates this to software's 'last 90%' and logistics' 'last mile' problems, noting that as long as something is unfinished, it remains potentially perfect.
标签: writing, creativity, productivity, procrastination, perfectionism, software development, psychology, finishing

item.title: Pluralistic: The Big Con (19 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Cory Doctorow's 'The Big Con' explores how pyramid schemes like Amway operate as a 'big con' where every person the mark encounters is in on the scam. He draws parallels to modern American politics, arguing that figures like Trump and Musk are pyramid scheme swindlers who deceive the public about their wealth and success. Doctorow suggests that many CEOs are now realizing they've all been lying about AI's profitability, similar to Amway victims who think they're the only ones failing. The piece critiques the belief that large investments must be legitimate, using a Bugs Bunny analogy to illustrate the absurdity.
标签: pyramid scheme, big con, Amway, politics, Trump, Musk, AI, fraud, capitalism, deception

item.title: Quoting Sean Lynch 摘要
item.description:
Sean Lynch argues that the key advantage of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) over skills or CLI is its ability to isolate authentication flows outside the agent's context window, potentially removing it from the harness entirely. He suggests that the idealized form of MCP might simply be an auth gateway for APIs, which would still be a significant improvement.
标签: MCP, authentication, LLM, AI, API, security, context-window, agent, protocol, generative-ai
